steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks what is notable about Schmidt's behavior. The key detail is the narrator's phrase "while making it abundantly clear there was no appointment at all" — Schmidt fabricates an excuse to leave after the narrator's wife says she doesn't find art compelling. His exit is theatrical, not genuine.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Schmidt is only given to theatrical behavior when in the company of the narrator and his first wife': The text gives no information about how Schmidt behaves in other company. ✗
  • Choice B — 'Schmidt conveys his feelings about one of his dining companions without explicitly stating them': Schmidt never says he disapproves of the wife's view — he communicates it through wincing, sighing, and a fake exit. ✓
  • Choice C — 'Schmidt's absentmindedness regarding his schedule is uncharacteristic of him': The narrator makes clear the appointment is invented, not forgotten. Absentmindedness is not the point. ✗
  • Choice D — 'Schmidt's departure is occasioned by the resumption of a previous disagreement with the narrator's first wife about a particular painting': No prior disagreement is mentioned in the text. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
